channel.title: Pharma AI & Data Watch
channel.description: Curated AI, pharma, and regulatory science intelligence — updated weekly.

item.title: CHIP-AML22: a complex clinical trial in de novo pediatric AML patients, including a gemtuzumab ozogamicin randomization and targeted therapy with quizartinib in eligible subgroups, within the NOPHO-DB-SHIP consortium
item.description: CHIP-AML22 is a complex clinical trial evaluating treatment strategies in newly diagnosed pediatric acute myeloid leukemia (AML) patients through a multi-center consortium. The trial includes a randomized assessment of gemtuzumab ozogamicin and targeted therapy with quizartinib for eligible patient

item.title: Alphafold pioneer john jumper joins anthropic
item.description: New Delhi : Renowned scientist John Jumper announced his exit from Google DeepMind after nearly nine years to join Anthropic following a short break. Jumper shared the update on social media platform X on Friday, expressing gratitude for his long ass

item.title: Alphafold pioneer john jumper joins anthropic after deepmind exit
item.description: Renowned scientist John Jumper announced his exit from Google DeepMind after nearly nine years to join Anthropic following a short break. Jumper shared the update on social media platform X on Friday , expressing gratitude for his long association with DeepMi…

item.title: Nobel winner john jumper to leave google deepmind for anthropic
item.description: Jumper has been a key member of Google’s AI coding development team and his departure furthers strains the tech giant’s efforts to beat Anthropic, OpenAI and Elon Musk’s SpaceX in the race to build the most powerful AI models.

item.title: 'it’s no longer enough for an app to tell you what to do. people want to know why': fitness app fitbod's founder on the reason behind the AI fitness boom
item.description: Allen Chen talks all things Fitbod, including what’s coming in the coming months.

item.title: Claude fable 5 on bedrock requires sharing inference data with anthropic
item.description: Using Claude Fable 5 or Mythos 5 on Amazon Bedrock requires opting into provider_data_share, sending prompts and outputs to Anthropic for 30-day retention with human review. Previous Bedrock models kept inference data inside the AWS boundary. Three days after…

item.title: Should everyone be on glp-1s? what the science really tells us about cancer prevention and other benefits
item.description: The laundry list of possible health benefits from GLP-1 drugs seems to get longer by the day, from cancer prevention to combating addiction. But while there’s no question these medications show immense promise beyond their use for diabetes and weight loss, sc…

item.title: Hackers claim to leak stolen madison square garden data
item.description: Plus: Gay bars in San Francisco using face scanners, France quits Palantir, Apple plans to change its private email and more.

item.title: Nobel laureate John Jumper is leaving DeepMind for rival Anthropic
item.description: Jumper isn't the only big name leaving Google DeepMind.

item.title: US curbs anthropic AI access, raising global concerns
item.description: The Trump administration has restricted foreign access to Anthropic's latest AI models over security fears, forcing a global shutdown. Could the row hurt the Claude-maker's IPO plans and set a far-reaching precedent? Silicon Valley executives and global polic…

item.title: Tech industry defector in ny-12 gets boost from new super pac
item.description: Guardrails Alliance has already raised $5 million and yesterday launched its first ad buys for New York state Rep. Alex Bores, who is running to replace longtime Rep. Jerry Nadler. The post Tech Industry Defector in NY-12 Gets Boost From New Super PAC appeare…

item.title: Keysight’s chief technologist on the path to 6g
item.description: Balaji Raghothaman, Keysight’s chief technologist for 6G, discusses what it takes for the telecoms industry to move to 6G, from supporting AI workloads to integrating with satellites connectivity and driving enterprise use cases

item.title: The week that changed AI: inside trump’s anthropic crackdown, and how a phone call from amazon CEO andy jassy triggered the chaos
item.description: The fight over Anthropic’s Mythos model is rewriting the rules of AI regulation, with consequences for the trillion dollar startup, the AI industry, and global security.

item.title: How a new york primary wound up at the center of the AI storm
item.description: Politics / June 19, 2026 How a New York Primary Wound Up at the Center of the AI Storm Alex Bores is engulfed in a brutal (and super-expensive) battle over how technology will change work, life, and society. John Nichols S…

item.title: A brain-computer interface has, for the first time, restored fluent everyday speech to a person who had lost it — a man with als who had not spoken aloud in years until a small implant from the braingate clinical trial began translating his attempts to speak directly into computer-synthesised words, in a result researchers reported in june 2026
item.description: Casey Harrell can now talk for twelve hours at a stretch. Two years ago, he could not talk at all. The ALS he was diagnosed with several years earlier had progressively destroyed the motor neurons controlling the muscles of his face and throat, leaving him wi…

item.title: Healthcare AI has left the lab. now comes the hard part.
item.description: Artificial intelligence in healthcare has evolved past a simple test-and-learn exercise. AI tools are now embedded into clinical scheduling, drug dispensing, patient communications and diagnostic decision-making across the industry, according to an analysis a…

item.title: The week in maternal-pediatric health tech (june 18) — the drought reaches week five: FDA pediatric label wave
item.description: Venture capital has now sat out five straight weeks in maternal-pediatric health. The FDA has not. On June 12 the agency cleared the first disease-modifying drug for newly diagnosed pediatric type 1 diabetes and the first over-the-counter glucose monitor clea…

item.title: Explore midjourney medical for ai-powered health scanning
item.description: Explore Midjourney Medical for AI-powered health scanningthedailyvibecode.vercel.app

item.title: Adeno-Associated Virus Gene Therapy Translation: Lessons from Early Regulatory Meetings
item.description: This article examines key insights from early regulatory meetings on adeno-associated virus (AAV) gene therapy development, drawing on experiences from the Therapeutic Development Branch and collaborating institutions to inform best practices for translation of AAV-based therapeutic approaches.

item.title: A startup claims it broke through a bottleneck that’s holding back LLMs
item.description: Miami-based AI startup Subquadratic came out of stealth mode last month with a huge claim. It announced that it had solved a mathematical bottleneck that had been holding back large language models for almost a decade. The details were thin, and many people were unconvinced. But Subquadratic has sta

item.title: Improving the accuracy and generalizability of molecular property regression models with a substructure-substitution-rule-informed framework

item.title: Nobel laureate john jumper leaves google deepmind for anthropic
item.description: John Jumper, a prominent research scientist and Nobel Prize winner, has announced his departure from Google DeepMind to join the AI startup Anthropic. Jumper is widely recognized for his pioneering work on AlphaFold, an artificial intelligence system capable …

item.title: U.s. orders anthropic to disable claude fable 5, mythos 5 over security concerns
item.description: "Our understanding is that the government believes it has become aware of a method of bypassing, or 'jailbreaking' Fable 5" to identify minor software vulnerabilities in the model." The post U.S. Orders Anthropic to Disable Claude Fable 5, Mythos 5 Over Secur…

item.title: AI governance for marketing: why cmos own it now — the aideate blog
item.description: New AI disclosure laws are reshaping marketing. Learn why AI governance now sits with CMOs, and how to build compliance into your workflow.

item.title: Can europe build its own AI heavyweights to rival the US?
item.description: With AI dominated by US firms, Europe is pushing for more independence. New partnerships, investments and startups aim to boost the continent's digital sovereignty, but challenges around funding and regulation remain.

item.title: AI update, june 19, 2026: AI news and views from the past week
item.description: Catch up on select AI news and developments since Friday, June 12. Stay in the know. Read the full article at MarketingProfs

item.title: Shadow AI – a step too far, or an opportunity?
item.description: Businesses are facing a new challenge - shadow AI. Teams are unleashing the potential of AI, but IT isn’t always aware and that’s creating a real risk related to ‘shadow AI’.

item.title: Retailers want ai-generated ads exempt from EU transparency rules
item.description: Europe’s retailers would like the law to make an exception for them. AI-generated advertising should be carved out of the European Union’s incoming transparency rules, a retail association has argued, according to Reuters, taking aim at provisions that would …

item.title: Show hn: i built an 11-llm consensus engine to detect AI hallucination
item.description: The only Next.js SaaS boilerplate with 11 LLM providers in semantic consensus, HSP fail-closed gate (Patent Pending), and EU AI Act audit-grade compliance. Production code from api.quorum-ai.dev. -...

item.title: Frontiers | structural gaps in referral and treatment pathways for gambling-related harm: a systematic review of health system responses using the antecedents–decision–outcomes framework
item.description: Frontiers | Structural gaps in referral and treatment pathways for gambling-related harm: a systematic review of health system responses using the antecedents–decision–outcomes frameworkfrontiersin.org

item.title: The surprising ways fatherhood benefits men’s brains, health and relationships
item.description: <![CDATA[ Clinical psychologist Darby Saxbe highlights how fathers increasingly take part in daily child care and caregiving. She says this involvement supports healthier relationships and delivers cognitive benefits. ]]>

item.title: Why databricks customerlake just rewired the cdp space
item.description: Databricks CustomerLake eliminates the standalone CDP for lakehouse users. Here's what enterprise teams should evaluate before their next renewal. Continue reading...

item.title: Klick health adds 18 senior leaders to help life sciences brands move faster, think bigger, and deliver more meaningful health outcomes
item.description: Klick Health Adds 18 Senior Leaders to Help Life Sciences Brands Move Faster, Think Bigger, and Deliver More Meaningful Health Outcomesmorningstar.com

item.title: Encryption, spyware, and now mythos: history shows why cyber export control doesn't work | techcrunch
item.description: For the last 30 years, stopping the flow of cybersecurity-related software has proven to be ineffective. It's unclear why it would work now with Anthropic’s cybersecurity model Mythos.

item.title: Billionaire ambani wants AI in every call, app, and home | techcrunch
item.description: Billionaire Ambani wants AI in every call, app, and home | TechCrunchtechcrunch.com

item.title: The film about sam altman has been dropped by amazon mgm
item.description: Luca Guadagnino's film about OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, Artificial, has reportedly been dropped by Amazon MGM. The film, which stars Andrew Garfield and covers the rollercoaster five days in 2023 spanning Altman's termination and reinstatement as CEO, had been in…

item.title: The CEO of allbirds' new AI biz has a plan, but no employees | techcrunch
item.description: Call it a startup with a sole founder and a very large seed round, but what's next is less clear.

item.title: Vercel debuts eve open source agent framework, tries to fix shadow AI with passport
item.description: Cost premium of using AWS indirectly via Vercel is mitigated by more efficient use of compute resources, CTO claims

item.title: Devs in the trenches are stressed from the mandate to automate everything, but render thinks it can help
item.description: San Francisco plays host to hosting company's Localhost conference

item.title: Britain's privacy watchdog quits after 'poor judgment' admission
item.description: John Edwards says his position had become 'untenable' following investigation into conduct including inappropriate attempts at humor

item.title: Is Kaposi's sarcoma the end of the OX40/OX40L axis in atopic dermatitis?
item.description: The OX40/OX40L axis has shown durable activity in clinical development for atopic dermatitis with strong biological rationale, but questions have emerged regarding whether monotherapy benefits are sufficient compared to established and emerging treatment options. The development of this therapeutic

item.title: Systematic optimization of PD-L1 and CLDN18.2 CAR-T designs identifies a bicistronic dual-target, double-CD3ζ architecture with enhanced antitumor activity in gastric cancer
item.description: Researchers systematically optimized chimeric antigen receptor T-cell (CAR-T) designs targeting PD-L1 and CLDN18.2 to identify an enhanced architecture using a bicistronic construct with dual signaling domains (double-CD3ζ) for treating gastric cancer. This optimized dual-target, double-CD3ζ CAR-T d

item.title: Antony pegg: introducing coldfront: seamlessly uniting oltp, analytics and AI workloads on postgresql
item.description: Our team is excited to announce pgEdge ColdFront v1.0.0-beta1: open-source, transparent data tiering for PostgreSQL that unites OLTP, analytics and AI workloads, with no application code changes required. The headline feature: a fully writable cold tier. Jimm…

item.title: Why transparent AI matters for enterprise trust
item.description: Black box AI risks slowing adoption as enterprises demand transparency, governance and accountability.

item.title: What is artificial intelligence (AI)?
item.description: * AI’s impact comes from its breadth: the same pattern-recognition capabilities now power everything from fraud detection and medical imaging to personalization and content generation. * AI capability is advancing faster than AI reliability, making evaluatio…

item.title: France advances europe’s AI future with nvidia technologies
item.description: France laid out plans to advance local AI — from new AI factories and national compute capacity to open frontier models and industrial platforms. Now, that AI infrastructure is coming online. AI agents are running in production, startups are deploying applica…

item.title: Some states are forging ahead with AI regulations
item.description: Six months after President Donald Trump warned states not to regulate artificial intelligence, they are increasingly doing just that. Congress has stalled on producing federal regulation of artificial intelligence as states forge ahead and scrutinize how chat…

item.title: How inova modernized its data architecture to prepare for AI apps
item.description: Inova went through a data modernization effort to prepare the health system's data foundation to support the rollout of capabilities such as AI agents and ambient scribes.

item.title: Just 30% of financial firms use AI for customer retention
item.description: Enterprise artificial intelligence has moved past the pilot stage, but there are some challenges when it comes to gaining wider acceptance. The PYMNTS Intelligence report “Financial Services Pulls Ahead in the Enterprise AI Race” was based on a survey of 60 s…
